title: The Prelude
quote: The world we know now is far different from what it once used to be.
author: Toru__
content:
Players before lived in a world where they built houses that they would soon live comfortably in after decorating their interiors. People would make accidents and hurt other people but would forgive with open arms.

This era of peace was short-lived, as something wicked came.

With no justifiable reason, people began to disappear.

More, more, more, and more. Until everyone was gone.

Where did we go?

We found ourselves in a new world vastly different from ours, far more beautiful. Almost as if the looks were to be deceiving.

We had no recollection of what was before or who we were, with only a few being able to remember figments of memories from the past.

We called the incident;

The Null Event.

Because something must’ve happened if we weren't in control of our own memories.

Why couldn’t we remember? Why were we here? It all just makes no sense at all, until you realize.

It’s here.

It did all of this and now it’s watching.

title: The Garden
summary: A period of peace, prosperity, and fragile harmony.
content:
With our pasts erased, we started anew.

Lived prosperously with new people, new memories, new faces, and new feelings.

Strangers and rivals built houses side-by-side, without being hindered by their past.

It’s as if you handed a painter a new canvas to paint on.

However each artist has their own styles, and ambitions. As some craved far more than a peaceful life in a loving community, something more reckless.

The harmony in which was given felt oddly fragile, and delicate.

Every single moment is soft to the touch; it is as if it can break at any time.

The only two questions were;

What will break it?

And

Who will break it?

The peace and quiet were only lended to us as it is watching us with keen and close eyes, as if watching ants from outside of a terrarium.

It wishes for us to relish in the bliss of the moment to make breaking us hurt even more, because it is malice in its purest form.

Its malignity knows no bounds and the world shall bear witness to its cruelty.
author: Toru__

title: The Hunt
summary: The Entity commanded the world to paint itself red.
content:
The wicked spoke, and every syllable reeked of malice from the purest form.

The breath of wild came stubborn into the world, with it came its influence and its power.

With an invisible hand, it lit the ancient portals to the hellscape with a flick of the wrist.

It spoke to every one of us, one sentence.

"Paint the world red."

Its as if the very words flicked the switch of morality within all of us and reverted us to the primalistic senses we hold deep within ourselves.

No one was spared from the slaughter, be it family nor friends.

Everyone killed each other.

And it watched, because it loves us.

It breaks us and assembles us back whole but wrong because it loves us.

It loves to see us come undone by our own hands.

Days passed until the effect of its influence wore off or that it halted it.

The era of peace is now long gone from history now that the water within the tank has been stained with blood.

The beautiful landscape of nature has been ruined, and the players have tasted blood.

It finally shows us what it is in all of its vainglorious form.

The Bloodsoakened, The Influence, Redacted, The Entity.

All names just to barely grasp what it is, but evil is the simplest thing it can be described as.

As what God would strike down its believers for confronting its mistakes?

Everything it has done is far from a mistake, given all intentionality.

We are united by one value, which is our powerlessness under it.
author: Toru__

title: The Fracture
quote: The rift in the sky, opened with the corpse of a beloved.
summary: Reality itself began to break apart.
content:
With the corpse of a player who opposed it, the sky tore open and the portals from every stronghold lit up with chaotic energy from a forgotten realm.

The beast that guards the realm from within grew hungrier than it ever was before, its scales hardened, and its teeth sharpened.

Albeit the beast’s strength, we were able to slay and free the realm of its captor.

However, He, who is behind every tragedy, wasn't too keen on our victory.

As it was intended that we had died, that very step we took in the realm and rotted for others to heed as a warning to fear it and conform to it.

But now it was all too late, as it has grown bored of us and wishes to get rid of us one way or another.

By its own bloodsoaken hands or another hand of which that doesn’t belong to it.
